blocks.html_en: The appropriating vocabulary of technology has a long history. It is useful, because it performs a specific job. It has a purpose. We still talk about horsepower when we want to measure how powerful cars are. A workhorse is a reliable machine performing demanding tasks. If your laptop has top-of-the-line specs it is probably a beast. The machine sees; more precisely, a statistical function maps pixels to labels, but "computer vision" was easier to fund. The machine learns; more precisely, it compresses the recorded work of millions of people into a set of weights, but learning sounds like education, and education is not something you owe the textbook's authors for. The machine hallucinates; more precisely, the product is defective, but a defective product owes you a refund, while a mind that hallucinates asks for your patience. You feed data to a system, the system ingests data. A resource-hungry system consumes resources and digests information. AI is data-hungry and starved for compute. Neural, memory, reasoning, understanding: each borrowed word moves the object a little further out of the category of things machinistic, and a little further into the category of beings. The opposite, of course, has formed as well. Every era has described the mind in the image of its proudest machinery: hydraulic humours, clockwork, automaton, engine of reason... Now it is we who process, who lack bandwidth, who are hardwired, who need to reboot.

sentences.en: The appropriating vocabulary of technology has a long history.
sentences.zh: 技术词汇的使用有着悠久的历史。

sentences.en: It is useful, because it performs a specific job.
sentences.zh: 它很有用，因为它执行特定的工作。

sentences.en: It has a purpose.
sentences.zh: 它有一个目的。

sentences.en: We still talk about horsepower when we want to measure how powerful cars are.
sentences.zh: 当我们想要衡量汽车的动力时，我们仍然谈论马力。

sentences.en: A workhorse is a reliable machine performing demanding tasks.
sentences.zh: 主力是执行高要求任务的可靠机器。

sentences.en: If your laptop has top-of-the-line specs it is probably a beast.
sentences.zh: 如果您的笔记本电脑具有顶级规格，那么它可能是一台野兽。

sentences.en: The machine sees; more precisely, a statistical function maps pixels to labels, but "computer vision" was easier to fund.
sentences.zh: 机器看到；更准确地说，统计函数将像素映射到标签，但“计算机视觉”更容易获得资助。

sentences.en: The machine learns; more precisely, it compresses the recorded work of millions of people into a set of weights, but learning sounds like education, and education is not something you owe the textbook's authors for.
sentences.zh: 机器学习；更准确地说，它将数百万人的记录工作压缩为一组权重，但学习听起来像是教育，而教育并不是你欠教科书作者的东西。

sentences.en: The machine hallucinates; more precisely, the product is defective, but a defective product owes you a refund, while a mind that hallucinates asks for your patience.
sentences.zh: 机器产生幻觉；更准确地说，产品有缺陷，但是有缺陷的产品欠你退款，而产生幻觉的头脑需要你的耐心。

sentences.en: You feed data to a system, the system ingests data.
sentences.zh: 您向系统提供数据，系统摄取数据。

sentences.en: A resource-hungry system consumes resources and digests information.
sentences.zh: 资源匮乏的系统会消耗资源并消化信息。

sentences.en: AI is data-hungry and starved for compute.
sentences.zh: 人工智能需要数据和计算。

sentences.en: Neural, memory, reasoning, understanding: each borrowed word moves the object a little further out of the category of things machinistic, and a little further into the category of beings.
sentences.zh: 神经、记忆、推理、理解：每一个借用的词都将物体从机械事物的范畴中进一步移出一点，并进一步移入存在的范畴。

sentences.en: The opposite, of course, has formed as well.
sentences.zh: 当然，相反的情况也已经形成。

sentences.en: Every era has described the mind in the image of its proudest machinery: hydraulic humours, clockwork, automaton, engine of reason...
sentences.zh: 每个时代都以其最引以为傲的机器形象来描述心灵：液压体液、发条装置、自动机、理性引擎……

sentences.en: Now it is we who process, who lack bandwidth, who are hardwired, who need to reboot.
sentences.zh: 现在是我们在处理，我们缺乏带宽，我们是硬连线的，我们需要重新启动。

blocks.html_en: None of this would matter much if words were ornaments. But they are not, because words often become verdicts a priori. They settle quietly, before any argument has been made about ownership, accountability and liability. Which is to say, in our topic of technology and language, every technology poses two questions: who gets to define it, and who gets to use it. The first question (who gets to define it) is settled in funding proposals and product names and press releases, and the people who settle it are the same people who own the machines. The rest of us then (usually the ‘who’ in the ‘who gets to use it’) conduct the argument about use inside specific and narrow definitions. Presiding over the whole vocabulary is its largest and most profitable word, the one the industry cannot get through a paragraph without; Freedom. The tools, we are told, will make us free.

sentences.en: None of this would matter much if words were ornaments.
sentences.zh: 如果文字只是装饰品，那么这一切都无关紧要。

sentences.en: But they are not, because words often become verdicts a priori.
sentences.zh: 但事实并非如此，因为言语往往会成为先验的判断。

sentences.en: They settle quietly, before any argument has been made about ownership, accountability and liability.
sentences.zh: 在就所有权、责任和责任进行任何争论之前，他们会安静地解决。

sentences.en: Which is to say, in our topic of technology and language, every technology poses two questions: who gets to define it, and who gets to use it.
sentences.zh: 也就是说，在我们的技术和语言主题中，每种技术都会提出两个问题：谁来定义它，以及谁来使用它。

sentences.en: The first question (who gets to define it) is settled in funding proposals and product names and press releases, and the people who settle it are the same people who own the machines.
sentences.zh: 第一个问题（谁来定义它）是在资助提案、产品名称和新闻稿中解决的，解决这个问题的人就是拥有机器的人。

sentences.en: The rest of us then (usually the ‘who’ in the ‘who gets to use it’) conduct the argument about use inside specific and narrow definitions.
sentences.zh: 然后，我们其他人（通常是“谁可以使用它”中的“谁”）在具体和狭隘的定义内进行有关使用的争论。

sentences.en: Presiding over the whole vocabulary is its largest and most profitable word, the one the industry cannot get through a paragraph without; Freedom.
sentences.zh: 主持整个词汇是它最大、最赚钱的词，没有这个词，业界就无法读完一段话；自由。

sentences.en: The tools, we are told, will make us free.
sentences.zh: 我们被告知，这些工具将使我们获得自由。

blocks.html_en: Whether that specific promise of freedom means anything of substance depends on the actual meaning of freedom as a concept, and freedom turns out to be one of the most contested definitions in history. Its standard form comes from Isaiah Berlin in 1958. Negative freedom is the absence of interference: nobody stops you from doing a thing and so you carry on to do that thing. Positive freedom is the presence of capacity: you are actually able to lead the life you want, within reasonable constraints. Anglophone political culture, American culture above all, settled on the first (negative freedom) and let the second (positive freedom) fall into disuse. In that absence, freedom came to mean, almost by reflex, 'freedom from'.

sentences.en: Whether that specific promise of freedom means anything of substance depends on the actual meaning of freedom as a concept, and freedom turns out to be one of the most contested definitions in history.
sentences.zh: 这种对自由的具体承诺是否具有任何实质意义，取决于自由作为一个概念的实际含义，而自由是历史上最具争议的定义之一。

sentences.en: Its standard form comes from Isaiah Berlin in 1958.
sentences.zh: 它的标准形式来自于 1958 年的 Isaiah Berlin。

sentences.en: Negative freedom is the absence of interference: nobody stops you from doing a thing and so you carry on to do that thing.
sentences.zh: 消极自由是指没有干扰：没有人阻止你做某件事，所以你继续做那件事。

sentences.en: Positive freedom is the presence of capacity: you are actually able to lead the life you want, within reasonable constraints.
sentences.zh: 积极的自由是能力的存在：你实际上能够在合理的限制内过上你想要的生活。

sentences.en: Anglophone political culture, American culture above all, settled on the first (negative freedom) and let the second (positive freedom) fall into disuse.
sentences.zh: 英语国家的政治文化，尤其是美国文化，选择了第一个（消极自由），而放弃了第二个（积极自由）。

sentences.en: In that absence, freedom came to mean, almost by reflex, 'freedom from'.
sentences.zh: 在这种缺席的情况下，自由几乎是本能地意味着“免于”。

blocks.html_en: The historian Timothy Snyder, gives an account of this from the war on Ukraine. Drive the occupier out of a village and its people are, in the negative sense, free; nobody now prevents anything. But if there is no water, no passable road, no clinic and no school, nobody there is free in the full sense of the word. The medical staff who reached the concentration camps in 1945 reported the same thing in plain terms: opening the gates liberated no one. Months of care did. Freedom, then, is a set of conditions. It has to be built. It can only be built by people acting together. It is positive because it finally concerns what you would do and the things you actually care for, rather than the list of things nobody happened to prevent you from doing.

sentences.en: The historian Timothy Snyder, gives an account of this from the war on Ukraine.
sentences.zh: 历史学家蒂莫西·斯奈德从乌克兰战争中对此进行了描述。

sentences.en: Drive the occupier out of a village and its people are, in the negative sense, free; nobody now prevents anything.
sentences.zh: 将占领者赶出村庄，从消极意义上讲，村庄的人民就获得了自由；现在没有人阻止任何事情。

sentences.en: But if there is no water, no passable road, no clinic and no school, nobody there is free in the full sense of the word.
sentences.zh: 但如果没有水、没有通行的道路、没有诊所、没有学校，那么那里就没有人是真正意义上的自由。

sentences.en: The medical staff who reached the concentration camps in 1945 reported the same thing in plain terms: opening the gates liberated no one.
sentences.zh: 1945年到达集中营的医务人员简单地报告了同样的事情：打开大门没有释放任何人。

sentences.en: Months of care did.
sentences.zh: 几个月的护理做到了。

sentences.en: Freedom, then, is a set of conditions.
sentences.zh: 那么，自由是一系列条件。

sentences.en: It has to be built.
sentences.zh: 它必须被建造。

sentences.en: It can only be built by people acting together.
sentences.zh: 它只能由人们共同行动来建造。

sentences.en: It is positive because it finally concerns what you would do and the things you actually care for, rather than the list of things nobody happened to prevent you from doing.
sentences.zh: 它是积极的，因为它最终关系到你会做什么和你真正关心的事情，而不是没有人阻止你做的事情清单。

blocks.html_en: When David Graeber and David Wengrow did their research for The Dawn of Everything, they came to the conclusion that across most of human history freedom has meant three practical things: the freedom to move away, the freedom to refuse orders, and the freedom to remake the arrangements one lives under. Every one of these depends on conditions:

sentences.en: When David Graeber and David Wengrow did their research for The Dawn of Everything, they came to the conclusion that across most of human history freedom has meant three practical things: the freedom to move away, the freedom to refuse orders, and the freedom to remake the arrangements one lives under.
sentences.zh: 当大卫·格雷伯和大卫·温格罗为《万物的黎明》进行研究时，他们得出的结论是，在人类历史的大部分时间里，自由意味着三件实际的事情：离开的自由、拒绝命令的自由以及重新安排一个人生活的自由。

sentences.en: Every one of these depends on conditions:
sentences.zh: 其中每一项都取决于条件：

blocks.html_en: • You can leave only if somewhere else will receive you (the most basic form of freedom), which for most of history it was guaranteed by complex customs of hospitality. Early European settlers were astonished that colonists sometimes left European settlements to live among Indigenous peoples, while the reverse almost never happened.

sentences.en: You can leave only if somewhere else will receive you (the most basic form of freedom), which for most of history it was guaranteed by complex customs of hospitality.
sentences.zh: 只有当其他地方愿意接待你时你才可以离开（这是最基本的自由形式），在历史上的大部分时间里，这是由复杂的热情好客习俗所保证的。

sentences.en: Early European settlers were astonished that colonists sometimes left European settlements to live among Indigenous peoples, while the reverse almost never happened.
sentences.zh: 早期的欧洲定居者感到惊讶的是，殖民者有时会离开欧洲定居点，与土著人民一起生活，而相反的情况几乎从未发生过。

blocks.html_en: • You can refuse orders only if refusal will not be used against you. Decisions in a village often required lengthy discussion until consensus emerged. Authority existed, but it was situational and negotiable. A war leader could organize a military expedition. But outside the expedition, they had no authority over the everyday life of their crew.

sentences.en: You can refuse orders only if refusal will not be used against you.
sentences.zh: 仅当拒绝不会对您不利时，您才可以拒绝订单。

sentences.en: Decisions in a village often required lengthy discussion until consensus emerged.
sentences.zh: 村庄里的决定往往需要长时间的讨论才能达成共识。

sentences.en: Authority existed, but it was situational and negotiable.
sentences.zh: 权威是存在的，但它是视情况而定的，并且是可以协商的。

sentences.en: A war leader could organize a military expedition.
sentences.zh: 战争领袖可以组织军事远征。

sentences.en: But outside the expedition, they had no authority over the everyday life of their crew.
sentences.zh: 但在探险之外，他们无权干涉船员的日常生活。

blocks.html_en: • Remaking the arrangements one lives under is something no person can do alone, because a social form only exists if others agree to live inside it, and it becomes thinkable only where leaving and refusing remain open. This is the third freedom which resting on the other two, is the most demanding of all. Several Indigenous societies alternated between different forms of organization during the year. For example: During the summer, hundreds or thousands gathered in large settlements with formal leadership, ceremonies, and coordinated hunting. During the winter, they dispersed into small egalitarian bands where hierarchy disappeared. During drought years organization might had different and stricter forms.

sentences.en: Remaking the arrangements one lives under is something no person can do alone, because a social form only exists if others agree to live inside it, and it becomes thinkable only where leaving and refusing remain open.
sentences.zh: 改变一个人的生活安排是任何人都无法单独完成的事情，因为只有当其他人同意生活在其中时，一种社会形式才存在，并且只有在离开和拒绝保持开放的情况下，它才变得可以想象。

sentences.en: This is the third freedom which resting on the other two, is the most demanding of all.
sentences.zh: 这是第三种自由，它依赖于另外两种自由，也是所有自由中要求最高的。

sentences.en: Several Indigenous societies alternated between different forms of organization during the year.
sentences.zh: 一些原住民社团在这一年中交替采用不同的组织形式。

sentences.en: For example: During the summer, hundreds or thousands gathered in large settlements with formal leadership, ceremonies, and coordinated hunting.
sentences.zh: 例如：在夏季，数百或数千人聚集在大型定居点，有正式的领导、仪式和协调狩猎。

sentences.en: During the winter, they dispersed into small egalitarian bands where hierarchy disappeared.
sentences.zh: 冬天，他们分散成平等主义的小团体，等级制度消失了。

sentences.en: During drought years organization might had different and stricter forms.
sentences.zh: 在干旱年份，组织可能有不同的、更严格的形式。

blocks.html_en: These accounts give us a way to test the promise of freedom. If freedom is a set of conditions, built by people acting together, then no object can deliver freedom on its own, however capable the object is. A machine arrives into a household, a workplace, with a set of relations already arranged in someone's favor, and the best it is capable of doing is to act on the arrangement it finds. So when a technology is sold as freedom, the machine itself tells you very little about the meaning of the concept. What matters is the conditions waiting for it: who has the machine, who lacks the machine, and who it actually serves. And there are plenty of examples to draw from history.

sentences.en: These accounts give us a way to test the promise of freedom.
sentences.zh: 这些叙述为我们提供了一种检验自由承诺的方法。

sentences.en: If freedom is a set of conditions, built by people acting together, then no object can deliver freedom on its own, however capable the object is.
sentences.zh: 如果自由是由人们共同行动建立的一系列条件，那么任何物体都无法单独提供自由，无论该物体有多么强大。

sentences.en: A machine arrives into a household, a workplace, with a set of relations already arranged in someone's favor, and the best it is capable of doing is to act on the arrangement it finds.
sentences.zh: 一台机器进入一个家庭、一个工作场所，已经安排了一系列对某人有利的关系，它能做的最好的事情就是按照它发现的安排采取行动。

sentences.en: So when a technology is sold as freedom, the machine itself tells you very little about the meaning of the concept.
sentences.zh: 因此，当一项技术作为自由出售时，机器本身很少告诉你这个概念的含义。

sentences.en: What matters is the conditions waiting for it: who has the machine, who lacks the machine, and who it actually serves.
sentences.zh: 重要的是等待它的条件：谁拥有机器，谁缺乏机器，以及它实际上为谁服务。

sentences.en: And there are plenty of examples to draw from history.
sentences.zh: 历史上有很多例子可以借鉴。

sentences.en: Thor ad in The Spokesman Review, 1931
sentences.zh: 《发言人评论》中的雷神广告，1931 年

blocks.html_en: Through the early and middle decades of the last century, the washing machine was sold as a form of emancipation. Advertisements promised that the new electric appliances would release women from the worst of domestic labor. The boiling and scrubbing and wringing took whole days of the week. However the condition alone of having a working machine at home did not remove house labor. Across the decades in which washing machines, vacuum cleaners and refrigerators spread through ordinary homes, the hours women spent on housework stayed remarkably stable. The time-use studies Joann Vanek assembled in the 1970s found full-time housewives working around 52 hours a week in the mid-1920s and slightly more in the mid-1960s. This finding has been disputed by later researchers who found housework time did decline for some subgroups once better data controls were applied. The main story remains, that the standard of what counted as clean rose to meet the new capacity. The commercial laundries and the paid help (someone else's labor) fell away, and a great deal of work that had once been shared or sent out was quietly returned to a single person at home.

sentences.en: Through the early and middle decades of the last century, the washing machine was sold as a form of emancipation.
sentences.zh: 在上世纪初期和中期，洗衣机作为一种解放的形式被出售。

sentences.en: Advertisements promised that the new electric appliances would release women from the worst of domestic labor.
sentences.zh: 广告承诺，新的电器将使妇女摆脱最艰苦的家务劳动。

sentences.en: The boiling and scrubbing and wringing took whole days of the week.
sentences.zh: 煮沸、擦洗和绞干的过程需要一周的好几天。

sentences.en: However the condition alone of having a working machine at home did not remove house labor.
sentences.zh: 然而，仅在家中拥有一台工作机器这一条件并不能消除家务劳动。

sentences.en: Across the decades in which washing machines, vacuum cleaners and refrigerators spread through ordinary homes, the hours women spent on housework stayed remarkably stable.
sentences.zh: 在洗衣机、吸尘器和冰箱普及到普通家庭的几十年里，女性花在家务上的时间一直保持着相当稳定的状态。

sentences.en: The time-use studies Joann Vanek assembled in the 1970s found full-time housewives working around 52 hours a week in the mid-1920s and slightly more in the mid-1960s.
sentences.zh: Joann Vanek 在 20 世纪 70 年代进行的时间利用研究发现，20 年代中期全职家庭主妇每周工作大约 52 小时，而 1960 年代中期则略多一些。

sentences.en: This finding has been disputed by later researchers who found housework time did decline for some subgroups once better data controls were applied.
sentences.zh: 这一发现受到后来的研究人员的质疑，他们发现，一旦应用更好的数据控制，某些亚组的家务时间确实减少了。

sentences.en: The main story remains, that the standard of what counted as clean rose to meet the new capacity.
sentences.zh: 主要的故事仍然是，清洁的标准提高了以满足新的容量。

sentences.en: The commercial laundries and the paid help (someone else's labor) fell away, and a great deal of work that had once been shared or sent out was quietly returned to a single person at home.
sentences.zh: 商业洗衣店和有偿帮助（别人的劳动）消失了，大量曾经共享或发出的工作悄悄地回到了家里的一个人身上。

blocks.html_en: The promise had been liberation, but instead, what arrived was a specific rearrangement. Whether it felt like freedom depended almost entirely on your gender and class. A household with money met the machine very differently from the woman who now did everything alone, and to a higher standard than what a laundress had once been paid to do. The machine changed nobody's conditions. On the contrary, it landed on the conditions already in place and amplified them. Liberation was marketing speak, chosen by the people selling the appliance. The people living with it were never asked what they felt, they were only invited to pay for it.

sentences.en: The promise had been liberation, but instead, what arrived was a specific rearrangement.
sentences.zh: 承诺是解放，但到来的却是具体的重新安排。

sentences.en: Whether it felt like freedom depended almost entirely on your gender and class.
sentences.zh: 是否感觉自由几乎完全取决于你的性别和阶级。

sentences.en: A household with money met the machine very differently from the woman who now did everything alone, and to a higher standard than what a laundress had once been paid to do.
sentences.zh: 一个有钱的家庭对机器的认识与现在独自做所有事情的妇女截然不同，而且比以前雇洗衣女做的工作标准更高。

sentences.en: The machine changed nobody's conditions.
sentences.zh: 这台机器没有改变任何人的状况。

sentences.en: On the contrary, it landed on the conditions already in place and amplified them.
sentences.zh: 相反，它立足于已有的条件并放大了它们。

sentences.en: Liberation was marketing speak, chosen by the people selling the appliance.
sentences.zh: 解放是营销用语，由销售电器的人选择。

sentences.en: The people living with it were never asked what they felt, they were only invited to pay for it.
sentences.zh: 与它一起生活的人们从来没有被问过他们的感受，他们只是被邀请为此付出代价。

blocks.html_en: Nuclear power was to make electricity "too cheap to meter", in the 1954 words of the chairman of the US Atomic Energy Commission. The personal computer arrived under the banner of computer liberation (Ted Nelson's phrase from 1974). It was the people's machine against the corporate mainframe. The internet was declared independent of every government on earth in 1996, in a manifesto written, of all places, at Davos. Social media was credited for the uprisings of 2011 and briefly renamed liberation technology by a few. The gig platforms of the 2010s sold freedom as being your own boss, one ride at a time. Cryptocurrency promised to free money itself, and the unbanked along with it. Each was announced as emancipation from some drudgery or some gatekeeper. Each settled, within a decade or two, into a familiar shape: owned by few, worked by many, and marketed in the language of freedom.

sentences.en: Nuclear power was to make electricity "too cheap to meter", in the 1954 words of the chairman of the US Atomic Energy Commission.
sentences.zh: 用美国原子能委员会主席 1954 年的话来说，核电将使电力“太便宜而无法计量”。

sentences.en: The personal computer arrived under the banner of computer liberation (Ted Nelson's phrase from 1974).
sentences.zh: 个人电脑是在计算机解放的旗帜下问世的（泰德·尼尔森 1974 年的名言）。

sentences.en: It was the people's machine against the corporate mainframe.
sentences.zh: 这是对抗企业大型机的人民机器。

sentences.en: The internet was declared independent of every government on earth in 1996, in a manifesto written, of all places, at Davos.
sentences.zh: 1996 年，在达沃斯世界各地发表的一份宣言中，互联网被宣布独立于世界上每个政府。

sentences.en: Social media was credited for the uprisings of 2011 and briefly renamed liberation technology by a few.
sentences.zh: 社交媒体被认为促成了 2011 年的起义，并被少数人短暂地更名为“解放技术”。

sentences.en: The gig platforms of the 2010s sold freedom as being your own boss, one ride at a time.
sentences.zh: 2010 年代的演出平台以自己当老板、一次搭乘一次的方式兜售自由。

sentences.en: Cryptocurrency promised to free money itself, and the unbanked along with it.
sentences.zh: 加密货币承诺让金钱本身自由化，也让那些没有银行账户的人自由化。

sentences.en: Each was announced as emancipation from some drudgery or some gatekeeper.
sentences.zh: 每个人都被宣布从某种苦差事或某种看门人中解放出来。

sentences.en: Each settled, within a decade or two, into a familiar shape: owned by few, worked by many, and marketed in the language of freedom.
sentences.zh: 每一个都在十年或两年内形成了熟悉的形态：由少数人拥有，由许多人工作，并以自由的语言进行营销。

blocks.html_en: The pattern would be easier to resist if the promise of freedom was ever simply false. Each technology on that list did release somebody from something. The personal computer did put publishing into ordinary hands, and the social media platforms did, for a few years, carry news past state censors. The promise persuades because it begins from real experiences of a few people, and then extends those experiences on the assumption that they are everyone else’s experiences, with no evidence at all. And because each technology arrives under a fresh name and with no memory attached, the extension can be performed again each time as though it had never failed before.

sentences.en: The pattern would be easier to resist if the promise of freedom was ever simply false.
sentences.zh: 如果自由的承诺完全是错误的，那么这种模式就会更容易被抵制。

sentences.en: Each technology on that list did release somebody from something.
sentences.zh: 该列表中的每一项技术都确实使某人摆脱了某些事情。

sentences.en: The personal computer did put publishing into ordinary hands, and the social media platforms did, for a few years, carry news past state censors.
sentences.zh: 个人电脑确实让出版业进入了普通人的手中，社交媒体平台也确实在几年内让新闻通过了国家审查。

sentences.en: The promise persuades because it begins from real experiences of a few people, and then extends those experiences on the assumption that they are everyone else’s experiences, with no evidence at all.
sentences.zh: 这个承诺之所以具有说服力，是因为它从少数人的真实经历开始，然后在没有任何证据的情况下，假设这些经历是其他人的经历，从而延伸这些经历。

sentences.en: And because each technology arrives under a fresh name and with no memory attached, the extension can be performed again each time as though it had never failed before.
sentences.zh: 而且由于每种技术都以新名称出现并且没有附加内存，因此每次都可以再次执行扩展，就像以前从未失败过一样。

sentences.en: Ragebait as a marketing strategy
sentences.zh: 愤怒诱饵作为营销策略

blocks.html_en: The same promise is now being traced when observing the hyping of artificial intelligence. The tools free us from the dull and the repetitive, the new advocates say. They hand the keys of creation to anyone willing to just ask for them. What these advocates share is easier to hear in a single piece of testimony disconnected from the real world. A person explains that some new tool has set them free. They used it to clear away the parts of the job they found tedious. They built something over a weekend that would once have taken a month, and they shipped it. From this, they reach a conclusion about everyone else: technology is liberating, and what freed them will free the rest of us in turn.

sentences.en: The same promise is now being traced when observing the hyping of artificial intelligence.
sentences.zh: 当观察人工智能的炒作时，现在也可以看到同样的承诺。

sentences.en: The tools free us from the dull and the repetitive, the new advocates say.
sentences.zh: 新倡导者表示，这些工具让我们摆脱了枯燥和重复的工作。

sentences.en: They hand the keys of creation to anyone willing to just ask for them.
sentences.zh: 他们将创造的钥匙交给任何愿意索取的人。

sentences.en: What these advocates share is easier to hear in a single piece of testimony disconnected from the real world.
sentences.zh: 这些倡导者分享的内容在与现实世界脱节的单一证词中更容易听到。

sentences.en: A person explains that some new tool has set them free.
sentences.zh: 一个人解释说，一些新工具让他们获得了自由。

sentences.en: They used it to clear away the parts of the job they found tedious.
sentences.zh: 他们用它来清理他们认为乏味的工作部分。

sentences.en: They built something over a weekend that would once have taken a month, and they shipped it.
sentences.zh: 他们用一个周末的时间建造了一些以前需要一个月才能完成的东西，然后发货了。

sentences.en: From this, they reach a conclusion about everyone else: technology is liberating, and what freed them will free the rest of us in turn.
sentences.zh: 由此，他们得出了一个关于其他人的结论：技术正在解放，而解放了他们的东西也将反过来解放我们其他人。

blocks.html_en: That story holds only if you overlook a great deal that the person sharing this story also often leaves out. For example, years of accumulated technical knowledge, hardware that costs more than some people would be able to afford, time that can be spent on experimenting rather than on survival. And beneath all of it, a degree of authority over one's own working day that most people who work for a living never see. The ‘freedom’ on display belongs to someone who already controlled the conditions of their labor. The tool did not hand them that control. It assumed they already had it.

sentences.en: That story holds only if you overlook a great deal that the person sharing this story also often leaves out.
sentences.zh: 只有当你忽略了分享这个故事的人也经常遗漏的很多内容时，这个故事才成立。

sentences.en: For example, years of accumulated technical knowledge, hardware that costs more than some people would be able to afford, time that can be spent on experimenting rather than on survival.
sentences.zh: 例如，多年积累的技术知识、价格超出某些人承受能力的硬件、可以花在实验而不是生存上的时间。

sentences.en: And beneath all of it, a degree of authority over one's own working day that most people who work for a living never see.
sentences.zh: 而在这一切的背后，是对自己工作日的一定程度的权威，这是大多数谋生的人从未见过的。

sentences.en: The ‘freedom’ on display belongs to someone who already controlled the conditions of their labor.
sentences.zh: 所展示的“自由”属于那些已经控制了劳动条件的人。

sentences.en: The tool did not hand them that control.
sentences.zh: 该工具没有给他们控制权。

sentences.en: It assumed they already had it.
sentences.zh: 它假设他们已经拥有了它。

blocks.html_en: Wherever one person has privilege over another, the one with less privilege has to work constantly to understand the one with more privilege, to anticipate the moods, read the preferences, learn what pleases and what provokes (interpretive labor in Graeber’s terms). The person with privilege is excused from the exercise. They can afford to know almost nothing about the lives conducted beneath them, because nothing in their day forces them to look. Inequality distributes imagination unevenly.

sentences.en: Wherever one person has privilege over another, the one with less privilege has to work constantly to understand the one with more privilege, to anticipate the moods, read the preferences, learn what pleases and what provokes (interpretive labor in Graeber’s terms).
sentences.zh: 每当一个人比另一个人享有特权时，特权较小的人就必须不断努力去理解特权较多的人，预测情绪，了解偏好，了解什么令人高兴和什么令人兴奋（格雷伯术语中的解释劳动）。

sentences.en: The person with privilege is excused from the exercise.
sentences.zh: 有特权的人可以免于参加这项活动。

sentences.en: They can afford to know almost nothing about the lives conducted beneath them, because nothing in their day forces them to look.
sentences.zh: 他们对自己身下的生活几乎一无所知，因为在他们那个时代，没有什么强迫他们去看。

sentences.en: Inequality distributes imagination unevenly.
sentences.zh: 不平等使想象力分配不均。

blocks.html_en: The person generalizing from their weekend revelation with AI is drawing on that exemption (in the form of privilege) without noticing. They reason outward from their own command of the situation, and arrive at a confident claim about people whose relation to the same tool does not resemble their own at all. They are not required to imagine them, and so they simply don't. A worker in a corporation, a hospital back office, a call centre, a school, does not choose the system. The employer chooses it, installs it on hardware the worker will never own or inspect, and presents its use as a condition of staying employed, in some version of "use this, or account for why your numbers have fallen". The same object that can enlarge someone's agency, contracts then someone else’s, based on their own conditions.

sentences.en: The person generalizing from their weekend revelation with AI is drawing on that exemption (in the form of privilege) without noticing.
sentences.zh: 那些从周末对人工智能的启示中总结出来的人正在毫无察觉地利用了这种豁免（以特权的形式）。

sentences.en: They reason outward from their own command of the situation, and arrive at a confident claim about people whose relation to the same tool does not resemble their own at all.
sentences.zh: 他们根据自己对情况的掌控进行向外推理，并对那些与同一工具的关系与自己完全不同的人得出自信的主张。

sentences.en: They are not required to imagine them, and so they simply don't.
sentences.zh: 他们不需要想象它们，所以他们根本就没有想象。

sentences.en: A worker in a corporation, a hospital back office, a call centre, a school, does not choose the system.
sentences.zh: 公司、医院后台、呼叫中心、学校的工作人员不会选择该系统。

sentences.en: The employer chooses it, installs it on hardware the worker will never own or inspect, and presents its use as a condition of staying employed, in some version of "use this, or account for why your numbers have fallen".
sentences.zh: 雇主选择它，将其安装在工人永远不会拥有或检查的硬件上，并将其使用作为继续就业的条件，在某种版本的“使用它，或解释为什么你的数字下降”。

sentences.en: The same object that can enlarge someone's agency, contracts then someone else’s, based on their own conditions.
sentences.zh: 同一个物体可以扩大某人的代理权，然后根据自己的条件收缩其他人的代理权。

blocks.html_en: David Golumbia called this ideology "cyberlibertarianism". In the book he finished shortly before his death in 2023 he argued that the celebration of digital technology as inherently emancipating is a political programme presented as a neutral technical observation. This specific programme’s politics run consistently rightward even when the people repeating them do not think of themselves that way. The vocabulary gives it away. Freedom. Openness. Innovation. Disruption. Each word, in the advocate’s mouth, is conditioned with a practical demand, that the technology and its owners cannot be held accountable of any collective, democratic decision or critique. Golumbia called the resulting worldview "an incoherence in the service of power". It has no need to be consistent. It only needs to keep returning the same verdict that favors whoever already owns the infrastructure. Definition and ownership sit, as we see repeatedly throughout history, in the same hands.

sentences.en: David Golumbia called this ideology "cyberlibertarianism".
sentences.zh: 大卫·戈伦比亚（David Golumbia）将这种意识形态称为“网络自由主义”。

sentences.en: In the book he finished shortly before his death in 2023 he argued that the celebration of digital technology as inherently emancipating is a political programme presented as a neutral technical observation.
sentences.zh: 在他于 2023 年去世前不久完成的书中，他认为，对数字技术本质上解放的庆祝是一项以中立的技术观察形式呈现的政治计划。

sentences.en: This specific programme’s politics run consistently rightward even when the people repeating them do not think of themselves that way.
sentences.zh: 这个特定纲领的政治始终是右倾的，即使重复这些纲领的人并不这么认为自己。

sentences.en: The vocabulary gives it away.
sentences.zh: 词汇表泄露了这一点。

sentences.en: Freedom.
sentences.zh: 自由。

sentences.en: Openness.
sentences.zh: 开放性。

sentences.en: Innovation.
sentences.zh: 创新。

sentences.en: Disruption.
sentences.zh: 干扰。

sentences.en: Each word, in the advocate’s mouth, is conditioned with a practical demand, that the technology and its owners cannot be held accountable of any collective, democratic decision or critique.
sentences.zh: 倡导者嘴里的每一个字都以实际需求为条件，即技术及其所有者不能对任何集体的、民主的决定或批评负责。

sentences.en: Golumbia called the resulting worldview "an incoherence in the service of power".
sentences.zh: 哥伦比亚称由此产生的世界观是“为权力服务的不连贯”。

sentences.en: It has no need to be consistent.
sentences.zh: 它不需要保持一致。

sentences.en: It only needs to keep returning the same verdict that favors whoever already owns the infrastructure.
sentences.zh: 它只需要不断返回有利于已经拥有基础设施的人的相同判决。

sentences.en: Definition and ownership sit, as we see repeatedly throughout history, in the same hands.
sentences.zh: 正如我们在历史上反复看到的那样，定义和所有权掌握在同一个人手中。

blocks.html_en: One doesn’t need to be a libertarian to carry the ideology. A person can hold firm egalitarian commitments on housing, healthcare, labor, or who is allowed to cross a border, and then, the moment the subject turns to software, begin reciting the same marketing claims Elon Musk would make. Software is filed under a separate heading, a zone where the ordinary questions of who gains from and who decides about technology are suspended by agreement. This is Berlin's negative freedom pared down to its commercial minimum: freedom as the absence of anyone saying no.

sentences.en: One doesn’t need to be a libertarian to carry the ideology.
sentences.zh: 一个人并不需要成为一名自由主义者才能传播这种意识形态。

sentences.en: A person can hold firm egalitarian commitments on housing, healthcare, labor, or who is allowed to cross a border, and then, the moment the subject turns to software, begin reciting the same marketing claims Elon Musk would make.
sentences.zh: 一个人可以在住房、医疗保健、劳动力或允许跨境的问题上做出坚定的平等承诺，然后，当话题转向软件时，就开始背诵埃隆·马斯克(Elon Musk)会做出的同样的营销主张。

sentences.en: Software is filed under a separate heading, a zone where the ordinary questions of who gains from and who decides about technology are suspended by agreement.
sentences.zh: 软件被归档在一个单独的标题下，在这个区域中，谁从技术中受益以及谁决定技术的常见问题通过协议暂停。

sentences.en: This is Berlin's negative freedom pared down to its commercial minimum: freedom as the absence of anyone saying no.
sentences.zh: 这就是柏林的消极自由被削减到其商业最低限度：自由就是没有人说不。

blocks.html_en: If the technology were as self-evidently liberating as its advocates report, you would expect it to win on its merits. However, the standing to say no is being removed by design. Why a liberating technology would have that need? Why would a technology need to be absolved from critique? In December 2025 the government of the United States issued an executive order whose central purpose was to stop individual states from regulating artificial intelligence. It set up a litigation task-force inside the Department of Justice with the assigned job of suing states over their AI laws. It instructed that federal broadband money, funds meant to bring rural households online, to be withheld from states that declined to comply. It told federal regulators to find grounds for striking down state rules the administration had decided were "onerous". It arrived after an earlier, failed attempt to freeze all new state AI legislation for ten years, and alongside the same administration's stated view that the industry should not have to pay to use the copyrighted books and articles its models are trained on.

sentences.en: If the technology were as self-evidently liberating as its advocates report, you would expect it to win on its merits.
sentences.zh: 如果这项技术像其拥护者所描述的那样具有不言而喻的解放性，那么你就会期望它会因其优点而获胜。

sentences.en: However, the standing to say no is being removed by design.
sentences.zh: 然而，说“不”的立场正在被有意地取消。

sentences.en: Why a liberating technology would have that need?
sentences.zh: 为什么解放性的技术会有这样的需求？

sentences.en: Why would a technology need to be absolved from critique?
sentences.zh: 为什么一项技术需要免受批评？

sentences.en: In December 2025 the government of the United States issued an executive order whose central purpose was to stop individual states from regulating artificial intelligence.
sentences.zh: 2025年12月，美国政府发布了一项行政命令，其核心目的是阻止个别州监管人工智能。

sentences.en: It set up a litigation task-force inside the Department of Justice with the assigned job of suing states over their AI laws.
sentences.zh: 它在司法部内部设立了一个诉讼工作组，其任务是就各州的人工智能法律提起诉讼。

sentences.en: It instructed that federal broadband money, funds meant to bring rural households online, to be withheld from states that declined to comply.
sentences.zh: 它指示拒绝遵守规定的州将被扣留联邦宽带资金，即用于让农村家庭上网的资金。

sentences.en: It told federal regulators to find grounds for striking down state rules the administration had decided were "onerous".
sentences.zh: 它告诉联邦监管机构寻找理由废除政府认为“繁琐”的州规定。

sentences.en: It arrived after an earlier, failed attempt to freeze all new state AI legislation for ten years, and alongside the same administration's stated view that the industry should not have to pay to use the copyrighted books and articles its models are trained on.
sentences.zh: 在此之前，曾试图将所有新的国家人工智能立法冻结十年，但失败了，而且同一届政府还表示，该行业不应该付费使用受版权保护的书籍和文章来训练其模型。

blocks.html_en: Why would a technology that liberates need a litigation task-force to stop states from regulating it? Why would something that frees people require federal money to be withheld from those who question it? Why would a tool sold as empowerment need its own advocates to lobby? A thing that delivers what it promises does not usually need this much law bent in its favor. These are not the manoeuvres of a technology that wins arguments. They are the manoeuvres of a project that has to remove the public's capacity to refuse it before it can proceed. A great deal of what reaches us as technical inevitability turns out, once the funding and the legislation are set side by side, to be a sequence of decisions about people: who is exempted from which law, whose property may be taken without payment, which tier of government is permitted to ask a question. All of this is defended by appeal to intention, to the good the technology will eventually do. But there is a principle in systems thinking that reads as a universal law: the purpose of a system is what it does. Intentions fall away, because at scale they change nothing.

sentences.en: Why would a technology that liberates need a litigation task-force to stop states from regulating it?
sentences.zh: 为什么一项具有解放性的技术需要一个诉讼工作组来阻止国家对其进行监管？

sentences.en: Why would something that frees people require federal money to be withheld from those who question it?
sentences.zh: 为什么那些解放人民的东西需要联邦资金来阻止那些质疑它的人呢？

sentences.en: Why would a tool sold as empowerment need its own advocates to lobby?
sentences.zh: 为什么作为赋权出售的工具需要自己的倡导者进行游说？

sentences.en: A thing that delivers what it promises does not usually need this much law bent in its favor.
sentences.zh: 一个能够兑现其承诺的事物通常不需要如此多的法律对其有利。

sentences.en: These are not the manoeuvres of a technology that wins arguments.
sentences.zh: 这些并不是能够赢得争论的技术策略。

sentences.en: They are the manoeuvres of a project that has to remove the public's capacity to refuse it before it can proceed.
sentences.zh: 它们是一个项目的策略，必须消除公众拒绝它的能力，然后才能继续进行。

sentences.en: A great deal of what reaches us as technical inevitability turns out, once the funding and the legislation are set side by side, to be a sequence of decisions about people: who is exempted from which law, whose property may be taken without payment, which tier of government is permitted to ask a question.
sentences.zh: 一旦资金和立法同时制定，我们所得到的很多技术上的必然性就会变成一系列关于人的决定：谁可以免受哪项法律的约束，谁的财产可以被无偿拿走，哪一级政府被允许提出问题。

sentences.en: All of this is defended by appeal to intention, to the good the technology will eventually do.
sentences.zh: 所有这一切都是通过诉诸意图来捍卫的，即技术最终会带来好处。

sentences.en: But there is a principle in systems thinking that reads as a universal law: the purpose of a system is what it does.
sentences.zh: 但系统思维中有一个原则可以理解为普遍法则：系统的目的就是它的作用。

sentences.en: Intentions fall away, because at scale they change nothing.
sentences.zh: 意图消失了，因为从规模上看它们什么也改变不了。

blocks.html_en: So we look at what it does. It draws an enormous and rising amount of power. Data centers used roughly 415 terawatt-hours of electricity in 2024 and are on course to about double that by 2030, a quantity close to the entire annual electricity use of Japan. Through 2025 their demand grew by around 17 per cent, against roughly 3 per cent for global electricity demand as a whole. It draws water on a comparable scale, something near 800 billion litres in indirect consumption across American data centers in a single year. The money behind the build-out is not modest either, more than 400 billion dollars from five large technology firms in 2025, with larger figures already announced for this year. And at the base of it sits the cheap and extractive human labor that the story of effortless automation never includes: the people in lower-income countries paid under two dollars an hour to clean and label and filter the training data, some of them working through the most violent material on the internet so the finished product can come back ‘smooth’ and ‘clean’ to you and me.

sentences.en: So we look at what it does.
sentences.zh: 所以我们看看它的作用。

sentences.en: It draws an enormous and rising amount of power.
sentences.zh: 它消耗着巨大且不断增加的能量。

sentences.en: Data centers used roughly 415 terawatt-hours of electricity in 2024 and are on course to about double that by 2030, a quantity close to the entire annual electricity use of Japan.
sentences.zh: 2024 年，数据中心的用电量约为 415 太瓦时，到 2030 年，这一数字将增加一倍左右，接近日本全年的用电量。

sentences.en: Through 2025 their demand grew by around 17 per cent, against roughly 3 per cent for global electricity demand as a whole.
sentences.zh: 到 2025 年，他们的需求增长约 17%，而全球电力需求整体增长约 3%。

sentences.en: It draws water on a comparable scale, something near 800 billion litres in indirect consumption across American data centers in a single year.
sentences.zh: 它的取水规模相当，美国数据中心一年的间接消耗量接近 8000 亿升。

sentences.en: The money behind the build-out is not modest either, more than 400 billion dollars from five large technology firms in 2025, with larger figures already announced for this year.
sentences.zh: 扩建背后的资金也不小，到 2025 年，五家大型科技公司将投入超过 4000 亿美元，今年已经宣布了更大的数字。

sentences.en: And at the base of it sits the cheap and extractive human labor that the story of effortless automation never includes: the people in lower-income countries paid under two dollars an hour to clean and label and filter the training data, some of them working through the most violent material on the internet so the finished product can come back ‘smooth’ and ‘clean’ to you and me.
sentences.zh: 其基础是廉价且榨取性强的人力，而轻松自动化的故事从未包括在内：低收入国家的人们每小时支付不到两美元来清理、标记和过滤训练数据，其中一些人通过互联网上最暴力的材料进行工作，以便成品可以“顺利”和“干净”地返回给你和我。

blocks.html_en: Then there is what it does to the work itself, which follows the pattern the washing machine set. The work is not abolished, but rearranged. Hand the writing of documentation to a model and the human skill of writing documentation begins to weaken, because a skill that goes unpracticed decays. Stop recording the reasons behind a system's design, on the understanding that the model will regenerate it on request, and the memory of why anything was built in a specific manner begins to go, and with it the ability to change it on purpose. You see, a skill is a condition of freedom too. So is the recorded memory of why things were made the way they were. Strip both out of a workforce and you have not relieved it of drudgery; you have removed the conditions under which it could ever refuse anything. The capacity does not disappear when it leaves the worker. It moves to whoever owns the system. A workforce relieved of those capacities is easier to replace and cheaper to direct. None of this is incidental. At scale it is the whole of the effect.

sentences.en: Then there is what it does to the work itself, which follows the pattern the washing machine set.
sentences.zh: 然后是它对工作本身的影响，它遵循洗衣机设定的模式。

sentences.en: The work is not abolished, but rearranged.
sentences.zh: 这项工作并没有取消，而是重新安排。

sentences.en: Hand the writing of documentation to a model and the human skill of writing documentation begins to weaken, because a skill that goes unpracticed decays.
sentences.zh: 将文档的编写交给模型，编写文档的人类技能开始减弱，因为不练习的技能就会衰退。

sentences.en: Stop recording the reasons behind a system's design, on the understanding that the model will regenerate it on request, and the memory of why anything was built in a specific manner begins to go, and with it the ability to change it on purpose.
sentences.zh: 停止记录系统设计背后的原因，因为模型将根据请求重新生成它，并且关于为什么以特定方式构建任何东西的记忆开始消失，并且有目的地改变它的能力。

sentences.en: You see, a skill is a condition of freedom too.
sentences.zh: 你看，技能也是自由的一个条件。

sentences.en: So is the recorded memory of why things were made the way they were.
sentences.zh: 关于事物为何如此形成的记录记忆也是如此。

sentences.en: Strip both out of a workforce and you have not relieved it of drudgery; you have removed the conditions under which it could ever refuse anything.
sentences.zh: 将两者从劳动力中剔除，你并没有摆脱它的苦差事；你已经删除了它可以拒绝任何事情的条件。

sentences.en: The capacity does not disappear when it leaves the worker.
sentences.zh: 当它离开工人时，能力并不会消失。

sentences.en: It moves to whoever owns the system.
sentences.zh: 它转移到拥有该系统的任何人。

sentences.en: A workforce relieved of those capacities is easier to replace and cheaper to direct.
sentences.zh: 失去这些能力的劳动力更容易被替换，而且领导成本也更便宜。

sentences.en: None of this is incidental.
sentences.zh: 这一切都不是偶然的。

sentences.en: At scale it is the whole of the effect.
sentences.zh: 从规模上看，它是整个效果。

blocks.html_en: Against all this, an advocate will reach for history. Photography did not kill painting. Image software did not kill illustration. New tools have absorbed older crafts before and left them room to continue in altered forms. When it comes to AI though, this deployment is faster by a wide margin, it is aimed at the whole breadth of knowledge work at once rather than at any single trade, and it comes during a period of flat wages and weakened labor protections. This leaves the people it displaces far less to fall back on than a displaced craftsman had a hundred years ago.

sentences.en: Against all this, an advocate will reach for history.
sentences.zh: 反对这一切，倡导者将诉诸历史。

sentences.en: Photography did not kill painting.
sentences.zh: 摄影并没有杀死绘画。

sentences.en: Image software did not kill illustration.
sentences.zh: 图像软件并没有杀死插图。

sentences.en: New tools have absorbed older crafts before and left them room to continue in altered forms.
sentences.zh: 新工具吸收了以前的旧工艺，并为它们留下了继续改变形式的空间。

sentences.en: When it comes to AI though, this deployment is faster by a wide margin, it is aimed at the whole breadth of knowledge work at once rather than at any single trade, and it comes during a period of flat wages and weakened labor protections.
sentences.zh: 不过，就人工智能而言，这种部署速度要快得多，它针对的是整个知识工作范围，而不是任何单一行业，而且是在工资持平和劳动保护减弱的时期进行的。

sentences.en: This leaves the people it displaces far less to fall back on than a displaced craftsman had a hundred years ago.
sentences.zh: 这使得被取代的人们比一百年前流离失所的工匠要少得多的依靠。

sentences.en: Audre Lorde: Image by The Ethics Centre
sentences.zh: 奥德丽·洛德：图片由道德中心提供

blocks.html_en: The clearest analysis on record was delivered in September 1979, when Audre Lorde spoke at a New York conference marking thirty years of Simone de Beauvoir's The Second Sex. Audre had been invited late, and said so from the platform; she was one of two Black women on the programme, both placed on a single panel. The conference had otherwise managed to examine the condition of women without reference to poor women, Black women, or lesbians. During that event she drew the sentence that has circulated ever since: the master's tools will never dismantle the master's house. The sentence is usually quoted as if it referred to hammers, but her argument was about frameworks. A movement that conducts all of its thinking inside the concepts of the order it opposes, she said, may win the occasional round of the master's game, but it will never build anything the master did not already have plans for. And she added an observation close in spirit to Graeber's: the excluded are kept permanently busy learning the master's concerns, while the master learns nothing of theirs.

sentences.en: The clearest analysis on record was delivered in September 1979, when Audre Lorde spoke at a New York conference marking thirty years of Simone de Beauvoir's The Second Sex.
sentences.zh: 有记录以来最清晰的分析是在 1979 年 9 月发表的，当时奥黛丽·洛德 (Audre Lorde) 在纽约举行的纪念西蒙娜·德·波伏娃 (Simone de Beauvoir) 的《第二性》三十周年的会议上发表了讲话。

sentences.en: Audre had been invited late, and said so from the platform; she was one of two Black women on the programme, both placed on a single panel.
sentences.zh: 奥黛丽迟到了，是在讲台上这么说的。她是该节目中的两名黑人女性之一，她们都被安排在一个小组中。

sentences.en: The conference had otherwise managed to examine the condition of women without reference to poor women, Black women, or lesbians.
sentences.zh: 会议在其他方面成功地审查了妇女的状况，而没有提及贫困妇女、黑人妇女或女同性恋者。

sentences.en: During that event she drew the sentence that has circulated ever since: the master's tools will never dismantle the master's house.
sentences.zh: 在那次活动中，她画下了流传至今的一句话：主人的工具永远不会拆掉主人的房子。

sentences.en: The sentence is usually quoted as if it referred to hammers, but her argument was about frameworks.
sentences.zh: 这句话通常被引用，好像它指的是锤子，但她的论点是关于框架的。

sentences.en: A movement that conducts all of its thinking inside the concepts of the order it opposes, she said, may win the occasional round of the master's game, but it will never build anything the master did not already have plans for.
sentences.zh: 她说，一个在其反对的秩序概念中进行所有思考的运动可能会偶尔赢得大师游戏的一轮，但它永远不会建造任何大师尚未计划的东西。

sentences.en: And she added an observation close in spirit to Graeber's: the excluded are kept permanently busy learning the master's concerns, while the master learns nothing of theirs.
sentences.zh: 她还补充了一个与格雷伯的精神接近的观察结果：被排除在外的人永远忙于了解主人的担忧，而主人却对他们一无所知。

blocks.html_en: If we set today's predicament beside Audre's speech, what is distributed under the banner of democratized AI is access to the master's tools in the simplest terms available: models owned by a handful of firms, run on hardware the same firms control, rented out by the token, metered, priced, controlled, deprecated and repriced at the owner's discretion. The space where the work happens is the master's. You are invited in on a subscription. A rentier-rentee relationship. And the vocabulary in which you are asked to evaluate the arrangement is supplied by the same master. Accept that the product is an intelligence, that ingesting your work is learning, that its failures are hallucinations, and that freedom means you are free from owning anything of all that. Every sentence available to you has been arranged in advance, so that refusal comes out sounding like superstition and fear of the future.

sentences.en: If we set today's predicament beside Audre's speech, what is distributed under the banner of democratized AI is access to the master's tools in the simplest terms available: models owned by a handful of firms, run on hardware the same firms control, rented out by the token, metered, priced, controlled, deprecated and repriced at the owner's discretion.
sentences.zh: 如果我们把今天的困境放在奥德雷的演讲旁边，那么在民主化人工智能的旗帜下传播的就是以最简单的方式使用大师的工具：少数公司拥有的模型，在同一家公司控制的硬件上运行，通过代币出租，由所有者自行决定计量、定价、控制、弃用和重新定价。

sentences.en: The space where the work happens is the master's.
sentences.zh: 工作发生的空间是主人的。

sentences.en: You are invited in on a subscription.
sentences.zh: 邀请您订阅。

sentences.en: A rentier-rentee relationship.
sentences.zh: 驯鹿与租客的关系。

sentences.en: And the vocabulary in which you are asked to evaluate the arrangement is supplied by the same master.
sentences.zh: 要求您评价编曲的词汇也是由同一位大师提供的。

sentences.en: Accept that the product is an intelligence, that ingesting your work is learning, that its failures are hallucinations, and that freedom means you are free from owning anything of all that.
sentences.zh: 接受产品是一种智慧，接受你的工作是学习，它的失败是幻觉，自由意味着你不拥有这一切。

sentences.en: Every sentence available to you has been arranged in advance, so that refusal comes out sounding like superstition and fear of the future.
sentences.zh: 你能想到的每句话都是提前安排好的，所以拒绝听起来就像是迷信和对未来的恐惧。

blocks.html_en: Lorde's counsel was not in despair. What she recommended was interdependence among the people the structure had no place for, treating difference as a resource rather than a threat, and building the capacities the house was never going to grant them. For the purpose of this essay, she was describing conditions, made cooperatively by the people who need them and answerable to no master.

sentences.en: Lorde's counsel was not in despair.
sentences.zh: 洛德的建议并没有绝望。

sentences.en: What she recommended was interdependence among the people the structure had no place for, treating difference as a resource rather than a threat, and building the capacities the house was never going to grant them.
sentences.zh: 她建议的是，这座建筑无法容纳的人们之间要相互依赖，将差异视为一种资源而不是威胁，并培养房子永远不会授予他们的能力。

sentences.en: For the purpose of this essay, she was describing conditions, made cooperatively by the people who need them and answerable to no master.
sentences.zh: 为了这篇文章的目的，她描述的是由需要它们的人们合作创造的条件，而不是对任何主人负责。

blocks.html_en: The encouraging part of seeing this whole situation plainly is that inevitability was always the most fragile claim in the case (along the marketable notion of ‘freedom’) . Nothing here is a law of nature. Data centers are placed through planning decisions that can be fought, and in more than a few places have been fought and stopped. Models are trained on copyrighted work under a permission that is being contested in court right now. The exemptions the industry relies on were granted by people who can be lobbied, replaced, or voted out. A technology that needs this much political cover is a very very fragile technology.

sentences.en: The encouraging part of seeing this whole situation plainly is that inevitability was always the most fragile claim in the case (along the marketable notion of ‘freedom’) .
sentences.zh: 清楚地看到整个情况的令人鼓舞的部分是，不可避免性始终是案件中最脆弱的主张（以及“自由”的市场概念）。

sentences.en: Nothing here is a law of nature.
sentences.zh: 这里没有什么是自然法则。

sentences.en: Data centers are placed through planning decisions that can be fought, and in more than a few places have been fought and stopped.
sentences.zh: 数据中心是通过规划决策放置的，可以进行战斗，并且在不止几个地方已经进行了战斗和停止。

sentences.en: Models are trained on copyrighted work under a permission that is being contested in court right now.
sentences.zh: 模特们接受了受版权保护的作品的培训，但目前法庭上正在对这一许可进行争议。

sentences.en: The exemptions the industry relies on were granted by people who can be lobbied, replaced, or voted out.
sentences.zh: 该行业所依赖的豁免是由可以游说、替换或投票退出的人授予的。

sentences.en: A technology that needs this much political cover is a very very fragile technology.
sentences.zh: 需要这么多政治掩护的技术是非常非常脆弱的技术。

blocks.html_en: Refusal, then, does more than say no to this technology. It makes room for a different question, the question of what we would build instead and for whom. The aim is not to bring down a specific company and call it finished, because the incentive that produced that company would simply produce the next one. The aim is to alter the conditions and to build the kinds of mutual support that make the worst of these tools redundant, because people are no longer desperate enough to reach for them. That is freedom in the positive sense. It is unglamorous and it needs lobbying, activism, and policy design... None of that can be generated on request. All of it has to be made together, communally, which is the one method the industry has no product for.

sentences.en: Refusal, then, does more than say no to this technology.
sentences.zh: 那么，拒绝不仅仅是对这项技术说不。

sentences.en: It makes room for a different question, the question of what we would build instead and for whom.
sentences.zh: 它为一个不同的问题腾出了空间，即我们将构建什么以及为谁构建的问题。

sentences.en: The aim is not to bring down a specific company and call it finished, because the incentive that produced that company would simply produce the next one.
sentences.zh: 我们的目的并不是要搞垮一家特定的公司并称其已完蛋，因为产生该公司的激励措施只会产生下一家公司。

sentences.en: The aim is to alter the conditions and to build the kinds of mutual support that make the worst of these tools redundant, because people are no longer desperate enough to reach for them.
sentences.zh: 目的是改变条件并建立相互支持，使这些工具中最糟糕的工具变得多余，因为人们不再迫切需要使用它们。

sentences.en: That is freedom in the positive sense.
sentences.zh: 这就是积极意义上的自由。

sentences.en: It is unglamorous and it needs lobbying, activism, and policy design...
sentences.zh: 它毫无魅力，需要游说、积极行动和政策设计……

sentences.en: None of that can be generated on request.
sentences.zh: 这些都不能根据请求生成。

sentences.en: All of it has to be made together, communally, which is the one method the industry has no product for.
sentences.zh: 所有这些都必须共同制作，这是该行业没有产品的一种方法。

blocks.html_en: Which finally returns us, to who gets to use technology. An advocate hyping AI may have saved time while they used it, and their choises felt real. The error was never in the experience. It was in the transliteration from their experience to ours, the assumption that a preference revealed by a person who already held the knowledge, the hardware, the time, and the authority could stand in for the preferences of everyone else. Remove that, and what remains is the oldest and most easily forgotten fact about any of this. These are simply human arrangements. Particular people made them, in particular interests, through particular decisions. Arrangements assembled that way can be taken apart and assembled differently. The world the advocates describe is handed to us as the only one available. It is not. It is one settlement (among several that were always possible), presented as destiny so that we will stop searching for the rest.

sentences.en: Which finally returns us, to who gets to use technology.
sentences.zh: 这最终让我们回到谁可以使用技术的问题。

sentences.en: An advocate hyping AI may have saved time while they used it, and their choises felt real.
sentences.zh: 大力宣传人工智能的倡导者可能会在使用人工智能时节省时间，而且他们的选择感觉很真实。

sentences.en: The error was never in the experience.
sentences.zh: 错误从来没有出现在经验中。

sentences.en: It was in the transliteration from their experience to ours, the assumption that a preference revealed by a person who already held the knowledge, the hardware, the time, and the authority could stand in for the preferences of everyone else.
sentences.zh: 这是从他们的经验到我们的经验的音译，假设一个已经拥有知识、硬件、时间和权威的人所揭示的偏好可以代替其他人的偏好。

sentences.en: Remove that, and what remains is the oldest and most easily forgotten fact about any of this.
sentences.zh: 除去这一点，剩下的就是关于这一切的最古老、最容易被遗忘的事实。

sentences.en: These are simply human arrangements.
sentences.zh: 这些根本就是人为的安排。

sentences.en: Particular people made them, in particular interests, through particular decisions.
sentences.zh: 特定的人为了特定的利益，通过特定的决定做出了它们。

sentences.en: Arrangements assembled that way can be taken apart and assembled differently.
sentences.zh: 以这种方式组装的装置可以以不同的方式拆卸和组装。

sentences.en: The world the advocates describe is handed to us as the only one available.
sentences.zh: 倡导者所描述的世界是我们唯一可用的世界。

sentences.en: It is not.
sentences.zh: 它不是。

sentences.en: It is one settlement (among several that were always possible), presented as destiny so that we will stop searching for the rest.
sentences.zh: 这是一个解决方案（在几个总是可能的解决方案中），作为命运呈现，以便我们将停止寻找其余的。

blocks.html_en: Choosing otherwise begins, as boring as it sounds, with words. Call the model a product and its maker a manufacturer, and questions of liability come back into view. Call the training corpus what it is: other people's work, which is appropriated, and the question of compensation returns. Say plainly that a subscription to somebody else's machine is not a capacity of your own, and Lorde's words come back in their full force. Above all, reserve the word freedom for the thing itself: the built, shared conditions under which people can act on what they actually care for. Freedom has never once shipped as a feature in a technical product. It is made by people together or it is not made at all. The greatest historical lesson is that no social order is inevitable. The institutions we inhabit today are human creations, and therefore they can be remade, if we want it.

sentences.en: Choosing otherwise begins, as boring as it sounds, with words.
sentences.zh: 尽管听起来很无聊，但选择其他方式是从言语开始的。

sentences.en: Call the model a product and its maker a manufacturer, and questions of liability come back into view.
sentences.zh: 将模型称为产品，将其制造商称为制造商，责任问题又回到了人们的视野。

sentences.en: Call the training corpus what it is: other people's work, which is appropriated, and the question of compensation returns.
sentences.zh: 训练语料库到底是什么：别人的工作，被挪用，以及报酬回报的问题。

sentences.en: Say plainly that a subscription to somebody else's machine is not a capacity of your own, and Lorde's words come back in their full force.
sentences.zh: 坦白地说，订阅别人的机器并不属于你自己的能力，洛德的话再次得到了充分的体现。

sentences.en: Above all, reserve the word freedom for the thing itself: the built, shared conditions under which people can act on what they actually care for.
sentences.zh: 最重要的是，将自由这个词保留给事物本身：一种构建的、共享的条件，在这种条件下，人们可以按照他们真正关心的事情采取行动。

sentences.en: Freedom has never once shipped as a feature in a technical product.
sentences.zh: 自由从来没有作为技术产品的一个功能出现过。

sentences.en: It is made by people together or it is not made at all.
sentences.zh: 它是由人们共同制作的，或者根本就不是制作的。

sentences.en: The greatest historical lesson is that no social order is inevitable.
sentences.zh: 最大的历史教训是，没有任何社会秩序是不可避免的。

sentences.en: The institutions we inhabit today are human creations, and therefore they can be remade, if we want it.
sentences.zh: 我们今天所居住的机构是人类的创造，因此，如果我们愿意的话，它们可以被重建。

content_html_zh:
ai

人工智能和解放机器的古老承诺，来自洗衣机、个人电脑、互联网和社交媒体。

1955 年夏天，约翰·麦卡锡 (John McCarthy) 坐下来撰写一份资助提案。 他希望洛克菲勒基金会资助第二年在达特茅斯举办的一个研讨会，而他希望研讨会开幕的领域还没有名字。 明显的候选人受到了损害。 他已经共同编辑了一本名为《自动机研究》的书，并对该书名所吸引的论文感到失望。 控制论被接受，并被诺伯特·维纳所吸引，麦卡锡多年后承认，他宁愿不与诺伯特·维纳争论。 所以他创造了新的东西：人工智能。

提案提出十年后，约瑟夫·魏森鲍姆 (Joseph Weizenbaum) 在麻省理工学院开发了一个名为 ELIZA 的程序，该程序通过数百行代码模仿心理治疗师，将一个人的陈述重新排列成问题。 它什么也不明白，魏森鲍姆知道这一点，因为每一行都是他写的。 他的秘书看着他写这些。 但这并没有阻止她一度要求他离开房间，这样她就可以和伊丽莎私下交谈。 魏森鲍姆在其职业生涯余下的大部分时间里都在试图解释为什么那一刻让他感到震惊，其中最全面的是 1976 年的《计算机能力与人类理性》。

多年后，约翰·麦卡锡承认，没有人能够以一种明确区分机器做什么和不做什么的方式来定义智能。 “人工智能”这个词是一个针对资助者的标语，它的效果如此之好，以至于七十年来，它组织了数千亿美元、多项国家产业战略，以及数百万从未听说过达特茅斯研讨会或约翰·麦卡锡的人的日常演讲。

技术词汇的使用有着悠久的历史。 它很有用，因为它执行特定的工作。 它有一个目的。 当我们想要衡量汽车的动力时，我们仍然谈论马力。 主力是执行高要求任务的可靠机器。 如果您的笔记本电脑具有顶级规格，那么它可能是一台野兽。 机器看到；更准确地说，统计函数将像素映射到标签，但“计算机视觉”更容易获得资助。 机器学习；更准确地说，它将数百万人的记录工作压缩为一组权重，但学习听起来像是教育，而教育并不是你欠教科书作者的东西。 机器产生幻觉；更准确地说，产品有缺陷，但是有缺陷的产品欠你退款，而产生幻觉的头脑需要你的耐心。 您向系统提供数据，系统摄取数据。 资源匮乏的系统会消耗资源并消化信息。 人工智能需要数据和计算。 神经、记忆、推理、理解：每一个借用的词都将物体从机械事物的范畴中进一步移出一点，并进一步移入存在的范畴。 当然，相反的情况也已经形成。 每个时代都以其最引以为傲的机器形象来描述心灵：液压体液、发条装置、自动机、理性引擎…… 现在是我们在处理，我们缺乏带宽，我们是硬连线的，我们需要重新启动。

如果文字只是装饰品，那么这一切都无关紧要。 但事实并非如此，因为言语往往会成为先验的判断。 在就所有权、责任和责任进行任何争论之前，他们会安静地解决。 也就是说，在我们的技术和语言主题中，每种技术都会提出两个问题：谁来定义它，以及谁来使用它。 第一个问题（谁来定义它）是在资助提案、产品名称和新闻稿中解决的，解决这个问题的人就是拥有机器的人。 然后，我们其他人（通常是“谁可以使用它”中的“谁”）在具体和狭隘的定义内进行有关使用的争论。 主持整个词汇是它最大、最赚钱的词，没有这个词，业界就无法读完一段话；自由。 我们被告知，这些工具将使我们获得自由。

这种对自由的具体承诺是否具有任何实质意义，取决于自由作为一个概念的实际含义，而自由是历史上最具争议的定义之一。 它的标准形式来自于 1958 年的 Isaiah Berlin。 消极自由是指没有干扰：没有人阻止你做某件事，所以你继续做那件事。 积极的自由是能力的存在：你实际上能够在合理的限制内过上你想要的生活。 英语国家的政治文化，尤其是美国文化，选择了第一个（消极自由），而放弃了第二个（积极自由）。 在这种缺席的情况下，自由几乎是本能地意味着“免于”。

历史学家蒂莫西·斯奈德从乌克兰战争中对此进行了描述。 将占领者赶出村庄，从消极意义上讲，村庄的人民就获得了自由；现在没有人阻止任何事情。 但如果没有水、没有通行的道路、没有诊所、没有学校，那么那里就没有人是真正意义上的自由。 1945年到达集中营的医务人员简单地报告了同样的事情：打开大门没有释放任何人。 几个月的护理做到了。 那么，自由是一系列条件。 它必须被建造。 它只能由人们共同行动来建造。 它是积极的，因为它最终关系到你会做什么和你真正关心的事情，而不是没有人阻止你做的事情清单。

当大卫·格雷伯和大卫·温格罗为《万物的黎明》进行研究时，他们得出的结论是，在人类历史的大部分时间里，自由意味着三件实际的事情：离开的自由、拒绝命令的自由以及重新安排一个人生活的自由。 其中每一项都取决于条件：

  • 只有当其他地方愿意接待你时你才可以离开（这是最基本的自由形式），在历史上的大部分时间里，这是由复杂的热情好客习俗所保证的。 早期的欧洲定居者感到惊讶的是，殖民者有时会离开欧洲定居点，与土著人民一起生活，而相反的情况几乎从未发生过。
  • 仅当拒绝不会对您不利时，您才可以拒绝订单。 村庄里的决定往往需要长时间的讨论才能达成共识。 权威是存在的，但它是视情况而定的，并且是可以协商的。 战争领袖可以组织军事远征。 但在探险之外，他们无权干涉船员的日常生活。
  • 改变一个人的生活安排是任何人都无法单独完成的事情，因为只有当其他人同意生活在其中时，一种社会形式才存在，并且只有在离开和拒绝保持开放的情况下，它才变得可以想象。 这是第三种自由，它依赖于另外两种自由，也是所有自由中要求最高的。 一些原住民社团在这一年中交替采用不同的组织形式。 例如：在夏季，数百或数千人聚集在大型定居点，有正式的领导、仪式和协调狩猎。 冬天，他们分散成平等主义的小团体，等级制度消失了。 在干旱年份，组织可能有不同的、更严格的形式。

这些叙述为我们提供了一种检验自由承诺的方法。 如果自由是由人们共同行动建立的一系列条件，那么任何物体都无法单独提供自由，无论该物体有多么强大。 一台机器进入一个家庭、一个工作场所，已经安排了一系列对某人有利的关系，它能做的最好的事情就是按照它发现的安排采取行动。 因此，当一项技术作为自由出售时，机器本身很少告诉你这个概念的含义。 重要的是等待它的条件：谁拥有机器，谁缺乏机器，以及它实际上为谁服务。 历史上有很多例子可以借鉴。

在上世纪初期和中期，洗衣机作为一种解放的形式被出售。 广告承诺，新的电器将使妇女摆脱最艰苦的家务劳动。 煮沸、擦洗和绞干的过程需要一周的好几天。 然而，仅在家中拥有一台工作机器这一条件并不能消除家务劳动。 在洗衣机、吸尘器和冰箱普及到普通家庭的几十年里，女性花在家务上的时间一直保持着相当稳定的状态。 Joann Vanek 在 20 世纪 70 年代进行的时间利用研究发现，20 年代中期全职家庭主妇每周工作大约 52 小时，而 1960 年代中期则略多一些。 这一发现受到后来的研究人员的质疑，他们发现，一旦应用更好的数据控制，某些亚组的家务时间确实减少了。 主要的故事仍然是，清洁的标准提高了以满足新的容量。 商业洗衣店和有偿帮助（别人的劳动）消失了，大量曾经共享或发出的工作悄悄地回到了家里的一个人身上。

承诺是解放，但到来的却是具体的重新安排。 是否感觉自由几乎完全取决于你的性别和阶级。 一个有钱的家庭对机器的认识与现在独自做所有事情的妇女截然不同，而且比以前雇洗衣女做的工作标准更高。 这台机器没有改变任何人的状况。 相反，它立足于已有的条件并放大了它们。 解放是营销用语，由销售电器的人选择。 与它一起生活的人们从来没有被问过他们的感受，他们只是被邀请为此付出代价。

用美国原子能委员会主席 1954 年的话来说，核电将使电力“太便宜而无法计量”。 个人电脑是在计算机解放的旗帜下问世的（泰德·尼尔森 1974 年的名言）。 这是对抗企业大型机的人民机器。 1996 年，在达沃斯世界各地发表的一份宣言中，互联网被宣布独立于世界上每个政府。 社交媒体被认为促成了 2011 年的起义，并被少数人短暂地更名为“解放技术”。 2010 年代的演出平台以自己当老板、一次搭乘一次的方式兜售自由。 加密货币承诺让金钱本身自由化，也让那些没有银行账户的人自由化。 每个人都被宣布从某种苦差事或某种看门人中解放出来。 每一个都在十年或两年内形成了熟悉的形态：由少数人拥有，由许多人工作，并以自由的语言进行营销。

如果自由的承诺完全是错误的，那么这种模式就会更容易被抵制。 该列表中的每一项技术都确实使某人摆脱了某些事情。 个人电脑确实让出版业进入了普通人的手中，社交媒体平台也确实在几年内让新闻通过了国家审查。 这个承诺之所以具有说服力，是因为它从少数人的真实经历开始，然后在没有任何证据的情况下，假设这些经历是其他人的经历，从而延伸这些经历。 而且由于每种技术都以新名称出现并且没有附加内存，因此每次都可以再次执行扩展，就像以前从未失败过一样。

当观察人工智能的炒作时，现在也可以看到同样的承诺。 新倡导者表示，这些工具让我们摆脱了枯燥和重复的工作。 他们将创造的钥匙交给任何愿意索取的人。 这些倡导者分享的内容在与现实世界脱节的单一证词中更容易听到。 一个人解释说，一些新工具让他们获得了自由。 他们用它来清理他们认为乏味的工作部分。 他们用一个周末的时间建造了一些以前需要一个月才能完成的东西，然后发货了。 由此，他们得出了一个关于其他人的结论：技术正在解放，而解放了他们的东西也将反过来解放我们其他人。

只有当你忽略了分享这个故事的人也经常遗漏的很多内容时，这个故事才成立。 例如，多年积累的技术知识、价格超出某些人承受能力的硬件、可以花在实验而不是生存上的时间。 而在这一切的背后，是对自己工作日的一定程度的权威，这是大多数谋生的人从未见过的。 所展示的“自由”属于那些已经控制了劳动条件的人。 该工具没有给他们控制权。 它假设他们已经拥有了它。

每当一个人比另一个人享有特权时，特权较小的人就必须不断努力去理解特权较多的人，预测情绪，了解偏好，了解什么令人高兴和什么令人兴奋（格雷伯术语中的解释劳动）。 有特权的人可以免于参加这项活动。 他们对自己身下的生活几乎一无所知，因为在他们那个时代，没有什么强迫他们去看。 不平等使想象力分配不均。

那些从周末对人工智能的启示中总结出来的人正在毫无察觉地利用了这种豁免（以特权的形式）。 他们根据自己对情况的掌控进行向外推理，并对那些与同一工具的关系与自己完全不同的人得出自信的主张。 他们不需要想象它们，所以他们根本就没有想象。 公司、医院后台、呼叫中心、学校的工作人员不会选择该系统。 雇主选择它，将其安装在工人永远不会拥有或检查的硬件上，并将其使用作为继续就业的条件，在某种版本的“使用它，或解释为什么你的数字下降”。 同一个物体可以扩大某人的代理权，然后根据自己的条件收缩其他人的代理权。

大卫·戈伦比亚（David Golumbia）将这种意识形态称为“网络自由主义”。 在他于 2023 年去世前不久完成的书中，他认为，对数字技术本质上解放的庆祝是一项以中立的技术观察形式呈现的政治计划。 这个特定纲领的政治始终是右倾的，即使重复这些纲领的人并不这么认为自己。 词汇表泄露了这一点。 自由。 开放性。 创新。 干扰。 倡导者嘴里的每一个字都以实际需求为条件，即技术及其所有者不能对任何集体的、民主的决定或批评负责。 哥伦比亚称由此产生的世界观是“为权力服务的不连贯”。 它不需要保持一致。 它只需要不断返回有利于已经拥有基础设施的人的相同判决。 正如我们在历史上反复看到的那样，定义和所有权掌握在同一个人手中。

一个人并不需要成为一名自由主义者才能传播这种意识形态。 一个人可以在住房、医疗保健、劳动力或允许跨境的问题上做出坚定的平等承诺，然后，当话题转向软件时，就开始背诵埃隆·马斯克(Elon Musk)会做出的同样的营销主张。 软件被归档在一个单独的标题下，在这个区域中，谁从技术中受益以及谁决定技术的常见问题通过协议暂停。 这就是柏林的消极自由被削减到其商业最低限度：自由就是没有人说不。

如果这项技术像其拥护者所描述的那样具有不言而喻的解放性，那么你就会期望它会因其优点而获胜。 然而，说“不”的立场正在被有意地取消。 为什么解放性的技术会有这样的需求？ 为什么一项技术需要免受批评？ 2025年12月，美国政府发布了一项行政命令，其核心目的是阻止个别州监管人工智能。 它在司法部内部设立了一个诉讼工作组，其任务是就各州的人工智能法律提起诉讼。 它指示拒绝遵守规定的州将被扣留联邦宽带资金，即用于让农村家庭上网的资金。 它告诉联邦监管机构寻找理由废除政府认为“繁琐”的州规定。 在此之前，曾试图将所有新的国家人工智能立法冻结十年，但失败了，而且同一届政府还表示，该行业不应该付费使用受版权保护的书籍和文章来训练其模型。

为什么一项具有解放性的技术需要一个诉讼工作组来阻止国家对其进行监管？ 为什么那些解放人民的东西需要联邦资金来阻止那些质疑它的人呢？ 为什么作为赋权出售的工具需要自己的倡导者进行游说？ 一个能够兑现其承诺的事物通常不需要如此多的法律对其有利。 这些并不是能够赢得争论的技术策略。 它们是一个项目的策略，必须消除公众拒绝它的能力，然后才能继续进行。 一旦资金和立法同时制定，我们所得到的很多技术上的必然性就会变成一系列关于人的决定：谁可以免受哪项法律的约束，谁的财产可以被无偿拿走，哪一级政府被允许提出问题。 所有这一切都是通过诉诸意图来捍卫的，即技术最终会带来好处。 但系统思维中有一个原则可以理解为普遍法则：系统的目的就是它的作用。 意图消失了，因为从规模上看它们什么也改变不了。

所以我们看看它的作用。 它消耗着巨大且不断增加的能量。 2024 年，数据中心的用电量约为 415 太瓦时，到 2030 年，这一数字将增加一倍左右，接近日本全年的用电量。 到 2025 年，他们的需求增长约 17%，而全球电力需求整体增长约 3%。 它的取水规模相当，美国数据中心一年的间接消耗量接近 8000 亿升。 扩建背后的资金也不小，到 2025 年，五家大型科技公司将投入超过 4000 亿美元，今年已经宣布了更大的数字。 其基础是廉价且榨取性强的人力，而轻松自动化的故事从未包括在内：低收入国家的人们每小时支付不到两美元来清理、标记和过滤训练数据，其中一些人通过互联网上最暴力的材料进行工作，以便成品可以“顺利”和“干净”地返回给你和我。

然后是它对工作本身的影响，它遵循洗衣机设定的模式。 这项工作并没有取消，而是重新安排。 将文档的编写交给模型，编写文档的人类技能开始减弱，因为不练习的技能就会衰退。 停止记录系统设计背后的原因，因为模型将根据请求重新生成它，并且关于为什么以特定方式构建任何东西的记忆开始消失，并且有目的地改变它的能力。 你看，技能也是自由的一个条件。 关于事物为何如此形成的记录记忆也是如此。 将两者从劳动力中剔除，你并没有摆脱它的苦差事；你已经删除了它可以拒绝任何事情的条件。 当它离开工人时，能力并不会消失。 它转移到拥有该系统的任何人。 失去这些能力的劳动力更容易被替换，而且领导成本也更便宜。 这一切都不是偶然的。 从规模上看，它是整个效果。

反对这一切，倡导者将诉诸历史。 摄影并没有杀死绘画。 图像软件并没有杀死插图。 新工具吸收了以前的旧工艺，并为它们留下了继续改变形式的空间。 不过，就人工智能而言，这种部署速度要快得多，它针对的是整个知识工作范围，而不是任何单一行业，而且是在工资持平和劳动保护减弱的时期进行的。 这使得被取代的人们比一百年前流离失所的工匠要少得多的依靠。

有记录以来最清晰的分析是在 1979 年 9 月发表的，当时奥黛丽·洛德 (Audre Lorde) 在纽约举行的纪念西蒙娜·德·波伏娃 (Simone de Beauvoir) 的《第二性》三十周年的会议上发表了讲话。 奥黛丽迟到了，是在讲台上这么说的。她是该节目中的两名黑人女性之一，她们都被安排在一个小组中。 会议在其他方面成功地审查了妇女的状况，而没有提及贫困妇女、黑人妇女或女同性恋者。 在那次活动中，她画下了流传至今的一句话：主人的工具永远不会拆掉主人的房子。 这句话通常被引用，好像它指的是锤子，但她的论点是关于框架的。 她说，一个在其反对的秩序概念中进行所有思考的运动可能会偶尔赢得大师游戏的一轮，但它永远不会建造任何大师尚未计划的东西。 她还补充了一个与格雷伯的精神接近的观察结果：被排除在外的人永远忙于了解主人的担忧，而主人却对他们一无所知。

如果我们把今天的困境放在奥德雷的演讲旁边，那么在民主化人工智能的旗帜下传播的就是以最简单的方式使用大师的工具：少数公司拥有的模型，在同一家公司控制的硬件上运行，通过代币出租，由所有者自行决定计量、定价、控制、弃用和重新定价。 工作发生的空间是主人的。 邀请您订阅。 驯鹿与租客的关系。 要求您评价编曲的词汇也是由同一位大师提供的。 接受产品是一种智慧，接受你的工作是学习，它的失败是幻觉，自由意味着你不拥有这一切。 你能想到的每句话都是提前安排好的，所以拒绝听起来就像是迷信和对未来的恐惧。

洛德的建议并没有绝望。 她建议的是，这座建筑无法容纳的人们之间要相互依赖，将差异视为一种资源而不是威胁，并培养房子永远不会授予他们的能力。 为了这篇文章的目的，她描述的是由需要它们的人们合作创造的条件，而不是对任何主人负责。

清楚地看到整个情况的令人鼓舞的部分是，不可避免性始终是案件中最脆弱的主张（以及“自由”的市场概念）。 这里没有什么是自然法则。 数据中心是通过规划决策放置的，可以进行战斗，并且在不止几个地方已经进行了战斗和停止。 模特们接受了受版权保护的作品的培训，但目前法庭上正在对这一许可进行争议。 该行业所依赖的豁免是由可以游说、替换或投票退出的人授予的。 需要这么多政治掩护的技术是非常非常脆弱的技术。

那么，拒绝不仅仅是对这项技术说不。 它为一个不同的问题腾出了空间，即我们将构建什么以及为谁构建的问题。 我们的目的并不是要搞垮一家特定的公司并称其已完蛋，因为产生该公司的激励措施只会产生下一家公司。 目的是改变条件并建立相互支持，使这些工具中最糟糕的工具变得多余，因为人们不再迫切需要使用它们。 这就是积极意义上的自由。 它毫无魅力，需要游说、积极行动和政策设计…… 这些都不能根据请求生成。 所有这些都必须共同制作，这是该行业没有产品的一种方法。

这最终让我们回到谁可以使用技术的问题。 大力宣传人工智能的倡导者可能会在使用人工智能时节省时间，而且他们的选择感觉很真实。 错误从来没有出现在经验中。 这是从他们的经验到我们的经验的音译，假设一个已经拥有知识、硬件、时间和权威的人所揭示的偏好可以代替其他人的偏好。 除去这一点，剩下的就是关于这一切的最古老、最容易被遗忘的事实。 这些根本就是人为的安排。 特定的人为了特定的利益，通过特定的决定做出了它们。 以这种方式组装的装置可以以不同的方式拆卸和组装。 倡导者所描述的世界是我们唯一可用的世界。 它不是。 这是一个解决方案（在几个总是可能的解决方案中），作为命运呈现，以便我们将停止寻找其余的。

尽管听起来很无聊，但选择其他方式是从言语开始的。 将模型称为产品，将其制造商称为制造商，责任问题又回到了人们的视野。 训练语料库到底是什么：别人的工作，被挪用，以及报酬回报的问题。 坦白地说，订阅别人的机器并不属于你自己的能力，洛德的话再次得到了充分的体现。 最重要的是，将自由这个词保留给事物本身：一种构建的、共享的条件，在这种条件下，人们可以按照他们真正关心的事情采取行动。 自由从来没有作为技术产品的一个功能出现过。 它是由人们共同制作的，或者根本就不是制作的。 最大的历史教训是，没有任何社会秩序是不可避免的。 我们今天所居住的机构是人类的创造，因此，如果我们愿意的话，它们可以被重建。
